content: Environmental health encompasses all aspects of human health determined by physical, chemical, biological, social, and psychosocial factors in the environment. Key environmental hazards include: (1) Air pollution—particulate matter (PM2.5, PM10), ozone, nitrogen dioxide, sulfur dioxide causing respiratory and cardiovascular disease; (2) Water contaminants—lead, arsenic, PFAS ('forever chemicals'), disinfection byproducts; (3) Toxic substances—asbestos, pesticides, heavy metals, endocrine disruptors; (4) Radiation—radon, UV radiation, medical imaging; (5) Built environment—housing quality, urban design, green space access. WHO estimates 24% of global deaths are attributable to environmental factors. Environmental justice examines disproportionate exposure among marginalized communities.
